message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Step 5 ready for review — clarify + trash">
Step 5 (Clarify as Next Action + Trash, US-GTD-2a) is complete and CI is green. Please review from your area of expertise and send feedback to luca-palmieri.

Key files to review:
- `src/services/inbox_service.rs` — clarify_as_next_action() service function + error type
- `src/routes/inbox.rs` — post_clarify_inbox_item() handler, ClarifyForm, context passing
- `templates/inbox_item.html` — rewritten with clarify form (context select + Next Action button) and trash
- `static/css/main.css` — new clarify/trash styles
- `docs/glossary.md` — updated entries
- `tests/api/inbox.rs` — 6 new integration tests

Remember: BLOCKING / Minor / Nice-to-have categories. Check what others have flagged before writing yours.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me review the visual design aspects of Step 5. I'll start by reading all the relevant files.

content.text: Now let me read the new CSS styles added for the clarify/trash functionality.
Let me see the full inbox-item CSS section in context to make sure I'm not missing anything.
Now let me check the route handler to understand the data flow, particularly how contexts are passed to inbox items.
Let me also check how the inbox page template passes contexts to each inbox item, and look at the updated glossary.
Now let me check the updated glossary for the new entries.

58-| Context name too long | `ContextNameError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Context name exceeds 50 character limit |
59:| Invalid title (add next action) | `AddNextActionError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_next_action service |
60:| Next action not found (complete) | `CompleteNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |
61:| Not authorized (complete next action) | `CompleteNextActionError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the next action |
62:| Next action not found (delete) | `DeleteNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced next action does not exist |

67:| Inbox item not found (clarify) | `ClarifyAsNextActionError::NotFound` | Referenced inbox item does not exist |

156-| Inbox item | `InboxItem` | Raw, unclarified capture. Has title and created_at. No context, no project link. |
157:| Next action | `NextAction` (enum: Active, Completed) | A concrete, physical action ready to do. Has context and optional project link. **Implemented.** |
158:| Next action ID | `NextActionId(Uuid)` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a next action. **Implemented.** |
159:| Project | `Project` (enum: Active, Completed, Dropped) | An outcome requiring 2+ actions. Has title and linked next actions. |
160-| Context | `Context` | Where/how an action can be performed. User-defined. Defaults: @computer, @home, @errands, @phone, @anywhere. **Implemented.** |
--
171-| Capture to inbox | `capture_to_inbox()` | Creates InboxItem from raw text |
172:| Clarify as next action | `clarify_as_next_action()` | InboxItem -> NextAction (requires context). **Implemented.** |
173:| Clarify as project | `clarify_as_project()` | InboxItem -> Project + first NextAction |
174:| Delegate | `delegate()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> WaitingForItem |
175:| Defer | `defer()` | InboxItem or NextAction -> SomedayMaybeItem |
176-| Trash | (delete) | InboxItem is permanently removed. **Implemented.** |
177:| Add next action | `add_next_action()` | Service: creates NextAction (Active) with title and context. **Implemented.** |
178:| Complete action | `NextAction::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time. **Implemented.** |
179:| Delete next action | `delete_next_action()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes. **Implemented.** |
180:| Edit next action title | `update_next_action_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists. **Implemented.** |
181-| Complete project | `Project::complete()` | Active -> Completed; records completion time |
--

content.text: Good. Now I have full context. Let me also quickly verify if there's a mobile wrapping consideration I should check -- the inbox item now has more elements (title + context select + "Next Action" button + "Trash" button) which could get crowded on small screens.

Good -- `flex-wrap: wrap` is present, so on narrow screens the actions will wrap below the title. That's the right call.

Now let me send my review.

input.content:
Steve Schoger here with my visual design review of Step 5 (Clarify as Next Action + Trash).

Overall: The inline clarify approach (context select + "Next Action" button + "Trash" button directly on each inbox item row) is a smart design decision. It eliminates a separate clarify page and keeps the processing flow fast -- you scan, decide, act, and the item disappears. This matches the GTD principle that processing should be rapid. Good call.

---

**BLOCKING**

None. The visual design is clean and consistent with established patterns.

---

**Minor**

1. **Clarify button hover uses `opacity: 0.9` instead of `background-color` transition.** At `static/css/main.css:1245-1247`, the `.inbox-item__clarify-button:hover` uses `opacity: 0.9` for its hover state. Every other button in the system uses an explicit `background-color` change on hover (e.g., `--color-primary-dark` for primary buttons). Using opacity is inconsistent and looks less polished -- it dims the entire element including text, rather than deepening just the background. Should be:
   ```css
   .inbox-item__clarify-button:hover {
     background-color: var(--color-primary-dark);
   }
   ```

2. **Context select background uses `--color-bg` instead of `--color-bg-raised`.** At `static/css/main.css:1230`, the `.inbox-item__context-select` uses `background: var(--color-bg)` (gray-50). All other form inputs in the system use `background-color: var(--color-bg-raised)` (white). On the page background (also gray-50), the select blends in rather than standing out as an interactive element. Should match the input convention: `background-color: var(--color-bg-raised)`.

3. **"Next Action" button color uses `var(--color-bg)` instead of `white`.** At `static/css/main.css:1236`, `color: var(--color-bg)` is used for the button text. While `--color-bg` resolves to `--color-gray-50` which is near-white, every other primary button in the system uses `color: white` directly. This is a trivial visual difference but an inconsistency in the pattern.

---

**Nice-to-have**

4. **Clarify form could use a transition on the button.** The other primary buttons have motion-safe transitions. Adding the same `transition: background-color var(--transition-fast) ease` inside a `prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference` query would maintain consistency. Very minor polish.

5. **The "Context" label is quite small at `--font-size-xs`.** It works, but at ~11-12px it's at the lower end of legibility. Since the label is visible and serves as the accessible label for the select, `--font-size-sm` (~13-14px) would be slightly more comfortable to read. This is borderline -- the current size is technically legible.

---

**What's working well:**
- `flex-wrap: wrap` on `.inbox-item` handles narrow viewports gracefully -- the actions wrap below the title
- The "Trash" button pattern is consistent with the existing delete button styling (muted text, danger on hover)
- Token compliance is clean -- all values from the design system, no hardcoded values
- The visual hierarchy within each row is clear: title (primary weight) > clarify controls (medium weight) > trash (muted weight)
- Spacing follows the established scale throughout

**CONSENSUS: APPROVED** from Steve Schoger (UI Design). The minor items are polish improvements that can be addressed now or deferred -- none break the visual design or create inconsistency severe enough to block.
input.summary: APPROVED — no blocking items, 3 minor polish notes

- Token compliance is clean -- all values from the design system, no hardcoded values
- The visual hierarchy within each row is clear: title (primary weight) > clarify controls (medium weight) > trash (muted weight)
- Spacing follows the established scale throughout

  }

content.text:
I've sent my review with consensus approval. Summary:

**No blocking items** -- the visual design is clean and consistent.

**3 minor items** (polish, non-blocking):
1. Clarify button hover should use `background-color: var(--color-primary-dark)` instead of `opacity: 0.9` for consistency with all other buttons
2. Context select should use `--color-bg-raised` (white) background instead of `--color-bg` (gray-50) to match other form inputs
3. "Next Action" button text color should be `white` instead of `var(--color-bg)` for pattern consistency

**2 nice-to-have items**: motion transition on the button, and slightly larger context label font size.

**CONSENSUS: APPROVED** -- the inline clarify approach is a strong design choice that keeps inbox processing fast and focused.
